stem: A school's IT technician is briefing pupils on common security threats.
review_notes: Word bank includes distractors. Not every word is used.

parts.label: (a)
parts.prompt: Drag a word from the bank into each gap. Not every word is used.

part_config.text: An email pretending to be from the bank to trick the user into clicking a fake login link is called {{g1}}. Hostile software such as a virus or ransomware is collectively called {{g2}}. Tricking someone into giving up their password by pretending to be IT support is an example of {{g3}}. A {{g4}} blocks unwanted network traffic before it reaches the LAN. To check whether the school's defences actually work, the technician hires an outside team to attack the system in a controlled way — this is called {{g5}}.
